title: spex-brick-scene (a real multi-part LDraw model assembly, cached)
description: Written/read by unibrick/brickscene.py. A real multi-part assembly, parsed directly from a real, official LDraw *model* file's own type-1 placement lines (https://library.ldraw.org/library/official/models/*.ldr — e.g. car.ldr, pyramid.ldr). Records *where* each real part goes (file, color, translation, rotation matrix); does not itself contain resolved triangle geometry — each distinct placement's part is resolved separately via a spex-brick-mesh (see brickmesh.schema.json) and placed via unibrick/brickmesh.py's place_mesh(). Cached under unibrick/.ldraw-cache/scenes/. Like brickmesh.json, this isn't checked by crates/spex-cli/tests/schema_validation.rs (Python-only cache format, no Rust reader yet) — unibrick/brickscene.py's validate_scene() is the real structural check run against generated output.

sourceModel.description: The real LDraw model filename this scene was parsed from (e.g. "car.ldr"), relative to LDraw's official models/ folder.

sourceDescription.description: The real model's own descriptive title, from its first real `0 <description>` comment line (e.g. "Example Car for Demonstration of LDRAW Library") — null if missing/unparseable.

sourceAuthor.description: The real model's own `0 Author: ...` line, if present — null otherwise.

attribution.description: Human-readable provenance, including the honest note that (unlike the LDraw Parts Library) official model files carry no explicit CCAL 2.0/CC BY license header, so their redistribution status is unconfirmed rather than assumed — see brickscene.py's module docstring.

partFile.description: The real LDraw part filename placed here (e.g. "3005.dat"), resolved into geometry via brickmesh.get_or_resolve_mesh, not stored inline in this file.

colorCode.description: This placement's real, top-level requested LDraw color code — passed straight to brickmesh.get_or_resolve_mesh as that part's requestedColorCode.

translation.description: This placement's real position, in the scene's own root LDU frame.

matrix.description: This placement's real 3x3 rotation/scale matrix (row-major, flattened to 9 numbers) — LDraw's own real type-1 line format. A real model routinely rotates placed parts (e.g. car.ldr's wheels), not just translates them, unlike gen_monolith_demo.py's hand-written stack.

buildStep.description: Real 0-indexed build-stage counter, incremented once per real `0 STEP` line encountered before this placement in the source model file. Not consumed by anything yet — kept because it's real, free provenance from the source file, not fabricated.
